item.title: The 438th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps
item.description:
This week I am promoting an online friend's Twitch Stream and Cat Surgery Fundraiser @ElbowDeepInPackages (Amazon shipments, not genitals) Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/erosrogziel She will be starting her streaming this week around 6-7 pm EST Game Schedule: Mon- Wild Card Day Tue- Stardew Valley Wed- Wild Card Day Thu- Risk Of Rain 2 Fri- Terraria Earlier Times, varied: Sat- Shellshock Sun- Valorant Fundraiser: 3 DAYS LEFT! https://www.paypal.com/pools/c/93Wy48Im0r The catto needs a semi-expensive tooth extraction, about $900 USD, more if they find more problems once it's removed. She's Currently at $490 / $900
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild *every other* Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you whenever one of my dumps goes Viral. If you wish to chat, I'm frequently in ElbowDeepInAHorse and PotatoCheesMonk's Discord Servers between the hours of 7pm and 1am GMT-5 during the week, and at unpredictable but frequent times Saturdays and Sundays.
Me. I'm the Dragon.
Me. I'm the Dragon. I collect things.
Worth it
Makes me think of something @ElbowDeepInAHorse would do for us.
Cheese
I disagree with Dr Pepper's placement
RIP that cake
My addition at the bottom
100% Fresh-Squeezed PyroDragon OC!
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Shoutout to my fellow Dumpers: @DeafKitten @CodyBurkett @OhValleyOfPlenty @AbbiiStabbii @DigiT00l @MemeMuseumCurator @CeleryMan7 @DumpsterKoala @JebidiahBeetus @Kaeldra @KirbyVictorious @HermioneDanger00 @DumpReview @WhiskeyJelly @JasonPositivityPilot @danat101 @PotatoCheesMonk @VanessaNelson @NotMuchBiggerThanAWomprat @MindNumbingBraIncAndy @TheMellowMoron @TheWoodsAreVile @ThatOtherGirlYouKnow @IStealMemesAndStuff @DrWalters @DaymanAhAAAAah @SecretHarpy Lemme know If you are a Regular Dumper and would like to be added to the list! Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #Phone_Dump #Meme_Dump #Memes #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple #GregFromHR #SteveFromAccounting #DougFromSales

item.title: The Ancient Texts pt-12
item.description:
Subtitle: A Phone Clearing Dump Been a minute gang. Welcome back to June 2019, what a time to be alive.
Asked my dog about this. His silence said everything...
I turn on the trailing path to max and I've had multiple people think my computer is broken. I purposely turn it on on work computers now just to fuck with people.
See also Canada *loosens coller nervously*
Anyone remember Leo and Satan?
Al... phonse... Play now?
New COD school edition
Fiance did this first year Engineering. Madwoman.
Strive for maximum efficiency workers.
Always be supportive when friends are discussing coming out. It is a challenging time.
I currently live in one of the Canadian Territories. Y'all got NOTHING on me! Below -50C it's too cold to even snow bro you have no idea. It's a fun place tho above -20 I recommend visiting at least once.
Respect
Judge recently ruled a burrito is a sandwich. LOOK IT UP!!
Yum
Vine 2 isn't as good and has suspicious privacy concerns change my mind.
Deus Vault!
It was a simpler time.
"weow you guys must really like Halloween"
Bb
Spread as you will

item.title: It's yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
most of the internet is in there
a sight to see
definition of unhinged
this + heat it up + inject into veins
i'm stumped
i guess
mmm hmm
i love american psycho. energy drinks, not so much
as is tradition
hell yeah, capri sun and apple slices
i didn't make this tweet, i just laughed at it. don't worry about me gang
i love pizza and the beach but i'm not a fan of getting sand in my food
gotta wonder what the number 2 magazine for pharrell's mom is like
i beg your pardon?
yes
better do what it says
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: No human, we're staying in bed
item.dc:creator: MrMrsCrowley

item.title: Blocking A Fire Truck
item.description: A fire engine was forced to stop responding to an emergency when an oncoming motorist crossed the double yellow line, drove head-on toward the pumper, blocked it in, and refused to move before eventually colliding with it. The driver, who was operating a van which resembled an undercover police vehicle, even had the audacity to post his video (below) on his social media page which highlights bad drivers. During the daytime of June 2, 2022, the Mombasha Fire Department in Monroe, NY was dispatched for natural gas actively leaking. This was considered a serious call since gas leaks cause explosions. The fire apparatus had pulled out of the station and was traveling on Route 17M with its red lights flashing and its siren and air horn steadily blaring when the driver apparently tried to provoke them. In the video, the fire engine can clearly be seen and heard pulling out of their station. As they begin pulling out, the oncoming driver, who identified himself as Billy Bolatti on social media, begins crossing the double yellow line and blocks the center of the road. As the engine attempts to maneuver around Bolatti, he continues driving forward. The engine then stops in the middle of the road, followed by Bolatti stopping facing it. As the fire apparatus sits stopped with the air horn emitting a long blast, Bolatti again starts driving head-on toward the rig. Bolatti stopped just a few feet from the engine’s front bumper. At that point, the engine could not go anywhere. A voice could be heard screaming at Bolatti to back up. He refused to comply. The driver of the pumper made one last attempt to maneuver around Bolatti, but the two vehicles collided. Bolatti could then be heard on video blowing his horn and screaming. Nobody was injured during the collision. According to a statement issued by the Mombasha Fire Company, Bolatti remained “extremely uncooperative” and “belligerent” after the collision. “The actions of the other motorist that continued after the collision prevented our apparatus and firefighters from responding to the emergency,” stated a written statement issued by the Mombasha Fire Company. Another rig had to be sent to cover the first emergency. The Monroe Police Department was called to the scene and took further action against the driver, although specific details are not available. Bolatti was operating a grayish van that somewhat resembled an undercover police vehicle, but is not. In another photo obtained on social media, the front license plate was removed and replaced with a red-on-black tag that stated “Essential Worker”. The hubcaps were removed. Two LED spotlights were mounted near each side of the windshield. The rear, side windows were heavily tinted. Fire department officials explained that the yellow stripes in the middle of the road are identified as “safety zones,” which act as imaginary boundaries. It is against traffic laws for motorists to drive over those lines. Emergency vehicles are permitted to travel in those zones when responding to an emergency. Traffic laws also mandate drivers to pull over and stop for an emergency vehicle, even if it is oncoming in the opposite lane. Bolatti apparently posts videos on different social media sites highlighting bad drivers in New York State. He claims that he was simply trying to make a left turn and the fire engine “rammed” him. The sheer audacity of the idiot blocking the fire truck to honk the horn and say "AY!" is simply incredible. This moron actually has a website that shows videos of bad drivers and he posted this video to it!!!! The fire truck should have hit him and plowed him off of the road.
item.dc:creator: Mangomania33

item.title: Wanted (Comic)
item.dc:creator: shenanigansen

item.title: It took 38 years, but I finally have a big girl tool chest!
item.description:
A couple of posts ago I mentioned that I was having some sticker shock after ordering a tool chest from Lowes. Well, it arrived yesterday and today I spend three hours loading it up. @.@ NO REGRETS.
I've been tinkering with restorating antique typewriters, cash registers, radios and other miscellaneous stuff for only four or five years now. In that time, I've acquired something of an organizational bottleneck. My hobby, combined with being a homeowner, have resulted in me having a lot of "stuff" and not many spots to put it.
My main problem is that I don't like to buy new furniture, so I don't. I'm happy with my grandma's couch from the 80's, my other grandma's dresser from the 50's (probably earlier knowing her), and the random assortment of bookcases and shelves I've acquired via osmosis and/or family handmedowns. I'm pretty sure my house's style can be found under "undefined." Only recently, and reluctantly, have I bought some inexpensive shelving units that I used initially to put my typewriters and even more recently hijacked to hold my tools and supplies for my restorations. When I started working on cash registers, though, my workshop (formerly the dining room) became an absolute mess.
I've bought some cheap, assemble-yourself work benches to alleviate the problem... and the problem sort of just spread out.
A lot. Tool storage is just the first step, that much I can see now. I need to figure out a better place for my parts machines, or decide whether to strip them out and finally recycle the iron frames. I've got donor typewriters I need to do the same with, as well as some unfinished projects that just need to get done before my eyes get dazzled by the next shiny thing. If I find whoever made this mess I'll kick their ass, aye so says I. But for today, one very satisfying step in that process is done:
Organization! The tools and their batteries, together again!
Sockets!
Sharp cutty things and big dull smashy things!
Smaller smashy things, skinny cutty things, and a refurbished blinding thing because my garage has yet to experience the wonders of the Edison lightbulb.
More stuff...
Just the bits. Kudos to my dad for stocking me up on more than half of these tools back when I moved out on my own. <3
Organizing is fun.
Normal drivers vs. all the tiny drivers I need for finnicky cash register screws. Also, varying chisels for my toenails.
Pinchy, bendy, and snippy tools. Also some dental picks, for unexpected visitors.
It's nice to have a drawer for my random assortment of extra cash register parts. Locks, endcaps, keys, all that good stuff.
And lastly, the majority of my bench supplies. Brushes and grinding wheels for the rotary tool, polishes, lube, and all the extra stuff I decided I needed at some point. :D
If you ask me, I think I made a dent! There's more room to improve things but that will take more time and planning. The tool chest and top box were a hair over four hundred bucks, so for now I'm closing the wallet until I have a battle plan.
Until then, I'm gonna relax for the rest of the week. Yee. :3
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.description: For those of you that don’t believe in public education, “abysmal,” means really really bad.

item.title: Dem Dang Dogs!
item.description: BEST day ever.
item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.title: Defying the odds.
item.description:
In December our 14 year old girl Jaz had to have some teeth out due to an infection and had a blood test at the same time, as the vet was concerned that her lymph glands were slightly swollen. When her test results came back they revealed that her lymphocyte levels were elevated, and this coupled with the swollen glands and a further needle biopsy led the vet to conclude that there was a high probability that she had lymphoma. This news was totally unexpected and came as a huge shock. With chemotherapy (Which at her age we weren't willing to put her through) the vet said she might last for another six to twelve months. Without chemo, she probably wouldn't make it past February. We were told what signs and symptoms to look out for that would indicate that her health was declining, none of which have materialised as yet - she's still the same feisty, daft and lovable little rascal she's always been. Today, her fifteenth birthday, was a day we never thought we'd get to see and she's spent it being thoroughly pampered and spoiled. The belly rubs and treats have been plentiful. I don't know how long it will be before we have to say goodbye, but be it weeks or months we're making the most of whatever time we have left with her. Hug your pets people, especially if they're getting on in years.
Jaz chilling out on the sofa today after scoffing a birthday treat bowl of steak mince & veggies. EDIT: Front page & a bunch of accolades? Wow, thanks people - I was sure this would die in Usersub. If Jaz could speak she'd probably say "WTF is an Imgur, can I eat it?" Cheers everyone, you all rock.
item.dc:creator: PrincePiggo
